creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) As US weighs response to Chinese AI, industry urges against broad open-weight restrictions
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: AI companies, including Nvidia and Mistral, urge policymakers to avoid broad restrictions on open-weight AI models as Washington debates responses to Chinese AI and alleged model distillation.
contentSnippet: AI companies, including Nvidia and Mistral, urge policymakers to avoid broad restrictions on open-weight AI models as Washington debates responses to Chinese AI and alleged model distillation.

title: (No AI) You can’t ignore Google Zero anymore
author: David Pierce
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/VRG_VST_20260724_Site.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The web and Google [once had a deal](https://www.theverge.com/24067997/robots-txt-ai-text-file-web-crawlers-spiders): Google collects data and indexes webpages and in exchange sends oceans of traffic to websites. The deal wasn't perfect and certainly made Google more money than it made the websites, but it worked for a long time. Now, however, [the deal seems to be dead](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/07/20/technology/google-ai-open-web.html?unlocked_article_code=1.zVA.L7zf._L5VDoBJCS4f&smid=url-share). And the web is being forced to reckon with what happens when the Google traffic dries up.

On [this episode of _The Vergecast_](https://pod.link/vergecast), David and Nilay talk about the week's Google Zero news, including Reddit's interest in [getting out of an AI training deal](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/969104/reddit-might-cut-ties-with-google-as-traffic-drops), and the publishers considering blocking Google from crawling their sites altogether. (Nilay also takes a bit o …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/podcast/970735/google-zero-reddit-ai-publishers-vergecast)
contentSnippet:
The web and Google once had a deal: Google collects data and indexes webpages and in exchange sends oceans of traffic to websites. The deal wasn't perfect and certainly made Google more money than it made the websites, but it worked for a long time. Now, however, the deal seems to be dead. And the web is being forced to reckon with what happens when the Google traffic dries up.
On this episode of The Vergecast, David and Nilay talk about the week's Google Zero news, including Reddit's interest in getting out of an AI training deal, and the publishers considering blocking Google from crawling their sites altogether. (Nilay also takes a bit o …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Anthropic releases Opus 5 with ‘close’ to Fable 5’s capabilities
author: Hayden Field
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/STKB364_CLAUDE_D.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Weeks after Anthropic's latest toe-to-toe with the US government, and days after an OpenAI [security incident](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/968988/openai-hugging-face-hack-ai) that dominated tech industry discussions, Anthropic on Thursday released its newest model, Claude Opus 5.

The company said in a release that Opus 5 "comes close to the capabilities of Claude Fable 5 in many domains" and is much better at complex coding tasks. (Fable 5 is the public-facing Mythos-class model that drew the government's ire, was [taken offline](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/950412/anthropic-trump-adminstration-claude-mythos-fable-5-export-controls) for a few weeks along with Mythos 5, and then [brought back](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/958964/anthropic-claude-fable-5-is-back) with even stronger cyber safeguards than before.)

The Fable 5 concerns - and the ensuing weeks of negotiations between …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/970105/claude-opus-5-announced-anthropic-ai-model-release)

The company said in a release that Opus 5 "comes close to the capabilities of Claude Fable 5 in many domains" and is much better at complex coding tasks. (Fable 5 is the public-facing Mythos-class model that drew the government's ire, was taken offline for a few weeks along with Mythos 5, and then brought back with even stronger cyber safeguards than before.) 
The Fable 5 concerns - and the ensuing weeks of negotiations between …

creator: Elana Klein
title: (No AI) Some Kids Will Never Think AI Is Cool
dc:creator: Elana Klein
content: “I think it should stand for artificial idiot,” one 9-year-old says. Here’s why kids of all ages are calling AI “disgusting” and “creepy.”
